alerts.title: Israel escalates Lebanon strikes despite ceasefire; 47+ killed in southern regions
alerts.summary: BBC, Al Jazeera, NBC News report Israeli airstrikes continuing in southern Lebanon despite announced ceasefire, with Lebanese officials reporting 47+ killed (lat 33.7-34.1°N, lon 35.5-35.7°E). Concurrent incidents feed reports Trump condemning Israel's Beirut attacks while Iran claims US responsibility, signaling ceasefire breakdown and imminent escalation risk.

alerts.title: US negative sentiment reaches 49%; Trump-Iran polarization deepens amid missile concession row
alerts.summary: US sentiment deteriorates to 42% positive / 49% negative (sentiment feed). Trump mentions spike to 86 (from 83), with 'war' (14), 'claims' (11), and 'begged' (10) keywords indicating domestic backlash over missile arsenal concession. Domestic political fracture now threatens unilateral deal withdrawal and escalation policy consistency.

alerts.title: US intelligence warns Israel likely to undermine Iran nuclear peace deal
alerts.summary: Washington Post reports US intelligence assessment that Israel is likely to systematically undermine Iran peace deal implementation (incidents feed). Concurrent with state department concerns over ceasefire violations in Lebanon, suggesting coordinated Israeli actions designed to collapse diplomatic framework and restart military operations.

alerts.title: Three US destroyers maintain Red Sea posture; carrier groups deployed across theater
alerts.summary: Assets feed confirms USS Carney, USS Mason, USS Laboon in Red Sea (15.3-15.7°N, 41.6-42.5°E); USS Dwight D. Eisenhower carrier group at 15.9°N, 42.0°E; USS Abraham Lincoln in Arabian Sea at 21.4°N, 60.5°E; USS Florida submarine in Persian Gulf (25.7°N, 51.9°E). Positioning reflects maximum deterrent posture amid ceasefire breakdown and Iranian threat rhetoric.
alerts.region: Red Sea / Persian Gulf / Arabian Sea

alerts.title: Iran enrichment stable at 60% U-235; disposal bottleneck complicates deal verification
alerts.summary: Global metrics confirm Iran maintaining 60% U-235 enrichment with 1-2 week breakout window. Jerusalem Post reports leaked MoU permits enriched uranium stockpile to remain in-country for dilution rather than export, creating technical disposal challenges and verification vulnerabilities. FDD analysis flags implementation bottleneck amid escalating political tensions.

analysis_note: Ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah is collapsing in real-time; concurrent US political fracture over Iran deal durability and Israeli intelligence assessment that Tel Aviv will sabotage nuclear agreement creates risk of synchronized military and diplomatic escalation across theater.
